title: Integration action usage / use webhook token to authenticate custom triggers
excerpt: Currently a custom trigger that is actually triggered by a webhook uses more integration actions from the customer account. One for the webhook, and one for each of the actual actions of the automation/integration. Monda…

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently a custom trigger that is actually triggered by a webhook uses more integration actions from the customer account. One for the webhook, and one for each of the actual actions of the automation/integration. Monday built-in triggers don’t require consuming an action to be triggered like custom do (assuming a monday source)

If we could use the JWT sent in a webhook header instead, there would be a way for monday to know the integration action for the webhook should be nullified as its was used to trigger a custom trigger which will then incur one (or more) automation/integration actions.

Built in triggers don’t use webhooks that consume account actions. This makes custom triggers more expensive for end users - and something they don’t understand.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for this request as well @anon29275264 !
